skill.name: image
skill.description: When the user wants to create, generate, edit, or optimize images for marketing — blog heroes, social graphics, product mockups, profile banners, listing visuals, or brand assets. Also use when the user mentions 'AI image generation,' 'generate an image,' 'create a graphic,' 'product mockup,' 'hero image,' 'social media graphic,' 'banner image,' 'cover photo,' 'profile banner,' 'listing screenshot,' 'Flux,' 'Flux Kontext,' 'Midjourney,' 'DALL-E,' 'GPT Image,' 'ChatGPT Images,' 'Ideogram,' 'Gemini image,' 'Nano Banana,' 'Recraft,' 'Stable Diffusion,' 'Canva,' 'Figma,' 'image optimization,' 'compress images,' 'WebP,' or 'OG image.' Use this for general-purpose marketing image creation and optimization. For paid ad image creative and platform-specific ad specs, see ad-creative. For video production, see video.
skill.summary: Create, generate, and optimize marketing images using AI tools like Flux, Midjourney, and Ideogram.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: The image skill is a content-only marketing documentation skill with no executable code. Static analysis flagged 92 patterns, but all are false positives: 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' matches are Markdown code blocks containing prompt templates, 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' matches are false positives triggered by words like 'compress' and 'optimize', and 'Screen capture upload' matches reference legitimate screenshot tools like screencapture CLI. No malicious intent detected.

content.value_statement: Creating professional marketing images is time-consuming and requires design expertise. This skill helps you generate, edit, and optimize visual assets using AI tools and best practices for any platform.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using AI models like Flux, Midjourney, Ideogram, and Gemini
Compare image generation models for text rendering, photorealism, and brand consistency
Create platform-specific dimensions for social media, blog heroes, and banners
Optimize images for web performance with WebP format and compression guidelines
Generate OG and social preview images with proper meta tags
Choose between AI generation, design tools, and stock photography approaches
limitations: Cannot execute image generation APIs directly — provides guidance for manual or API-based workflows
AI models hallucinate UI — not suitable for product mockup screenshots
Most AI models struggle with complex text rendering beyond short headlines
Logo design should use dedicated design tools, not AI generation

use_cases.title: Blog Hero Image Creation
use_cases.description: Generate unique hero images for blog posts using AI tools with proper dimensions and optimization.

use_cases.title: Social Media Graphics
use_cases.description: Create platform-specific images for Twitter, LinkedIn, Instagram with correct aspect ratios and sizes.

use_cases.title: Product Mockup Production
use_cases.description: Build product UI showcases using screenshots and design tools for directory listings and marketing.

prompt_templates.title: Blog Hero Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a blog hero image about [TOPIC]. Style: photorealistic, modern, clean. Setting: [CONTEXT]. Lighting: soft natural, shallow depth of field. Dimensions: 1200x630. Output as WebP, compressed under 200KB.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a blog post header image

prompt_templates.title: Social Media Graphic
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a [PLATFORM] social graphic for [CAMPAIGN]. Dimensions: [WIDTH]x[HEIGHT]. Include space for headline text overlay. Style: [BRAND STYLE]. Brand colors: [COLORS].

prompt_templates.title: OG Preview Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an OG image for [PAGE TITLE]. Size: 1200x630. Include: brand logo, page title text, visual element representing [TOPIC]. Ensure text is readable at small sizes.

prompt_templates.title: Product Directory Banner
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a directory listing banner for [PRODUCT NAME]. Dimensions: [PLATFORM SIZE]. Show: product UI screenshot with annotation. Style: clean, professional, brand-consistent.
prompt_templates.scenario: Product Hunt, G2, or marketplace listings

output_examples.input: I need a hero image for a blog post about productivity tips for remote teams.
output_examples.output: Recommended approach: AI generation with Flux or Gemini. Prompt: 'A diverse remote team collaborating via video calls, modern home office setting, warm natural lighting, photorealistic style, shallow depth of field, professional blog header aesthetic, 1200x630 dimensions.' Export as WebP at quality 80, target under 200KB.

output_examples.input: What size should my LinkedIn company cover image be?
output_examples.output: LinkedIn company cover: 1128x191 pixels (5.9:1 ratio). LinkedIn recommends up to 4200x700 for high-DPI displays. Keep critical content centered — edges get cropped differently across devices. Use brand colors with minimal text, as banners display small on mobile feeds.

output_examples.input: How do I optimize images for my landing page?
output_examples.output: Optimization checklist: 1) Serve WebP with JPEG fallback using <picture> element. 2) Resize to display dimensions — never serve oversized images. 3) Compress photos to 75-85% quality. 4) Add loading='lazy' for below-fold images. 5) Set explicit width/height to prevent layout shift. 6) Use a CDN with auto-optimization.

best_practices: Always specify dimensions and aspect ratio in your prompts to avoid cropping issues
Use Ideogram 3.0 for images containing text — most other models render text poorly
Optimize images to WebP format at quality 80 with JPEG fallback for broad browser support
anti_patterns: Using AI generation for product UI screenshots — models hallucinate interfaces, capture real screenshots instead
Skipping image optimization — unoptimized images are the leading cause of slow page loads
Generating logos with AI — results are inconsistent and not vector; use Figma or commission a designer

faq.question: Which AI model is best for generating marketing images?
faq.answer: For all-around use, Gemini Image (Nano Banana) offers editing and multi-image reference. For photorealism, use Flux Pro 1.1. For text-heavy graphics, Ideogram 3.0 is the best choice. For artistic quality, Midjourney v7 excels.

faq.question: What dimensions should I use for social media images?
faq.answer: Twitter/X: 1200x675. LinkedIn feed: 1200x627. Instagram feed: 1080x1080 or 1080x1350. Instagram Stories: 1080x1920. Facebook: 1200x630. Always check current platform specs before generating.

faq.question: How do I optimize images for web performance?
faq.answer: Serve WebP with JPEG fallback. Resize to display dimensions. Compress photos to 75-85% quality. Add loading='lazy' for below-fold images. Set explicit width and height attributes. Use a CDN with auto-optimization.

faq.question: Can AI generate product UI screenshots?
faq.answer: No. AI models hallucinate UI elements and produce inaccurate mockups. For product screenshots, capture real UI using browser DevTools, Shottr, or CleanShot X, then frame in device mockups.

faq.question: What is an OG image and why do I need one?
faq.answer: An OG (Open Graph) image appears when your URL is shared on social media, Slack, or Discord. Without one, shared links look broken. Standard size is 1200x630 pixels. Add meta tags for og:image, og:image:width, and og:image:height.

faq.question: Should I use AI or design tools for brand assets?
faq.answer: Use design tools (Figma, Canva) for logos, icons, and recurring templates. Use AI generation for unique hero images, lifestyle scenes, and creative visuals. AI works well for illustration concepts that you finalize in a design tool.
